content: The intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) from curiosity-driven agents can be reframed as a dynamic gating mechanism for active learning agent memory, where only unexpected, high-curiosity data samples are permanently encoded into long-term agent memory instead of storing all observed interaction data. This creates a self-organizing memory system that automatically prioritizes information that improves the agent's out-of-distribution generalization, aligning the agent's intrinsic exploration drive with the core active learning goal of maximizing model improvement with minimal annotation cost.
